lit: You shall not eat:
sm: You shall not eat:

syn.mc: Negative command within the quoted legal instruction.
syn.svo: Implied subject: ὑμεῖς. Verb: φάγεσθε.
syn.rhet: Abrupt prohibition closes the preceding ritual permissions.

lit: But whoever should eat by day
sm: But if anyone should eat by day
emd: Transmitted ἡ is syntactically impossible here; the clause is corrected to ὃς on contextual grounds and continues into line 343.

syn.mc: Protasis of a general conditional clause.
syn.sub: Dependent on the apodosis in line 343.
syn.svo: Subject: ὃς. Verb: φάγῃ. Adverbial dative: ἡμέρᾳ.
syn.rhet: The legal warning shifts from prohibition to conditional penalty.

lit: he should die by death.
sm: he shall surely die.
emd: Trailing OCR quotation mark normalized as punctuation.

syn.mc: Apodosis of the conditional sentence begun in line 342.
syn.sub: Dependent on the γενική condition ὃς δ᾽ ἂν ... φάγῃ.
syn.svo: Implied subject: ὃς. Verb: ἀποθανῇ. Dative of manner or penalty: θανάτῳ.
syn.rhet: The formulaic death penalty completes the legal warning.

src: ὃ δὲ ἄνθρωπος φύσει δεκτικὸς ὧν ἀγαθοῦ καὶ πονηροῦ,
ana: ὁ δὲ ἄνθρωπος φύσει δεκτικὸς ὧν ἀγαθοῦ καὶ πονηροῦ,
lit: But the man, by nature receptive of good and evil,
sm: But man, by nature capable of good and evil,
emd: Followed the article correction implied by the context: ὃ -> ὁ.

syn.mc: Nominal main clause beginning a description of fallen humanity.
syn.sub: The genitive phrase ὧν ἀγαθοῦ καὶ πονηροῦ is compressed and somewhat irregular, but its sense is clear in context.
syn.svo: Subject: ὁ ἄνθρωπος. Predicate adjective: δεκτικός. Adverbial dative: φύσει. Genitive complement: ὧν ἀγαθοῦ καὶ πονηροῦ.
syn.rhet: The sentence pivots from legal warning to anthropological explanation.

lit: like a clod of earth on both sides of seeds,
sm: like a clod of earth receptive of both seeds,

syn.mc: Comparative simile dependent on line 344.
syn.svo: No verb in the line; the noun phrase elaborates the simile.
syn.rhet: The human heart is likened to soil that can receive either seed.

src: ἐδέξατο τὸν ἐχθρὸν καὶ λίχνον σύμβουλον,"
ana: ἐδέξατο τὸν ἐχθρὸν καὶ λίχνον σύμβουλον,
lit: He received the enemy and the gluttonous counselor,
sm: he received the enemy and the greedy counselor,
emd: Trailing OCR quotation mark normalized as punctuation; line follows the legal/anthropological description of man.

syn.mc: Main clause completing the simile of the receptive human being.
syn.svo: Subject: ὁ ἄνθρωπος, implied. Verb: ἐδέξατο. Objects: τὸν ἐχθρὸν καὶ λίχνον σύμβουλον.
syn.rhet: The fall is dramatized as an act of hospitable reception toward evil.

src: καὶ προσαψάμενος τοῦ ξύλου παρέβη τὴν ἐντολὴν
tok: aor mid ptcp nom sg m
lit: And having touched the tree, he transgressed the commandment
sm: and, touching the tree, he transgressed the commandment

syn.mc: Main clause with attendant participle.
syn.sub: The participle προσαψάμενος supplies antecedent circumstance.
syn.svo: Subject: the human being, implied. Participle: προσαψάμενος. Verb: παρέβη. Object: τὴν ἐντολήν.
syn.rhet: The forbidden tree becomes the decisive point of transgression.

lit: And he disregarded God.
sm: and disobeyed God.

syn.mc: Coordinate main clause.
syn.svo: Subject: the human being, implied. Verb: παρήκουσεν. Object/genitive object: τοῦ θεοῦ.
syn.rhet: Transgression is restated as disobedience.

src: ἐξεβλήθη γοῦν εἰς τοῦτον τὸν κόσμον
lit: Therefore he was cast out into this world
sm: therefore he was cast out into this world

syn.mc: Main clause.
syn.svo: Subject: the human being, implied. Verb: ἐξεβλήθη. Prepositional phrase: εἰς τοῦτον τὸν κόσμον.
syn.rhet: Expulsion is the consequence of disobedience.

lit: as into a prison of condemned men.
sm: as into a prison for condemned men.

syn.mc: Comparative prepositional phrase dependent on line 349.
syn.svo: No verb in the line; the phrase elaborates the metaphor of the world as prison.
syn.rhet: The world is reimagined as a penal enclosure.

src: Τούτου δὲ πολυχόου καὶ πολυχρονίου γενομένου,
tok: aor mid ptcp gen sg m/n
lit: This having become <UNCERTAIN: obscure> and long-lasting,
sm: when this had become long-lasting and <UNCERTAIN: obscure>,
emd: The genitive absolute is secure, but πολυχόου is lexically obscure; I have left it as transmitted and marked it as uncertain.

syn.mc: Genitive absolute introducing the next causal developments.
syn.svo: Genitive subject: Τούτου. Participle: γενομένου. Coordinate adjectives: πολυχόου καὶ πολυχρονίου.
syn.ptc: γενομένου forms a genitive absolute; the adjectives characterize the transformed human condition.
syn.rhet: The result of transgression is rendered as a durative condition.

src: διὰ τῆς τοῦ ξύλου γεύσεως διαλυθέντος
tok: aor pass ptcp gen sg m/n
lit: through the tasting of the tree having been dissolved
sm: through the tasting of the tree, after it was dissolved,

syn.mc: Continuation of the genitive absolute chain from line 351.
syn.svo: Prepositional phrase: διὰ τῆς τοῦ ξύλου γεύσεως. Participle: διαλυθέντος.
syn.ptc: διαλυθέντος continues the genitive absolute, expressing the undoing of the human condition by transgression.
syn.rhet: The tree's taste becomes the means of dissolution.

tok: aor act ptcp gen sg m/n
lit: and having gone into earth,
sm: and having returned to earth,

syn.mc: Final member of the genitive absolute chain begun in line 351.
syn.svo: Participle: χωρήσαντος. Prepositional phrase: εἰς γῆν.
syn.ptc: The participle marks the downward movement or return to earth.
syn.rhet: The sequence moves from tasting to dissolution to earthward descent.

src: κατελείφθη ὑπ᾽ αὐτοῦ κληρονομία τοῖς τέκνοις αὐτοῦ
lit: An inheritance was left by him to his children
sm: An inheritance was left by him to his children,

syn.mc: Passive main clause.
syn.svo: Subject: κληρονομία. Verb: κατελείφθη. Agent: ὑπ᾽ αὐτοῦ. Indirect object: τοῖς τέκνοις αὐτοῦ.
syn.rhet: The inheritance is introduced first in the passive to underline its legacy-status.

lit: For he left an inheritance to his children
sm: for he left an inheritance to his children

syn.mc: Explanatory main clause.
syn.svo: Subject: he, implied. Verb: κατέλιπεν. Object: κληρονομίαν. Indirect object: τοῖς τέκνοις.
syn.rhet: The active restatement clarifies the passive claim of line 356.

lit: not chastity but fornication,
sm: not chastity but fornication,

syn.mc: Nominal item in the inheritance list.
syn.svo: Pair of object nouns after implied κληρονομίαν.
syn.rhet: The inheritance is defined by antithetical pairs.

lit: not incorruptibility but corruption,
sm: not incorruptibility but corruption,

syn.mc: Nominal item in the inheritance list.
syn.svo: Parallel pair of accusative nouns after the implied inheritance.
syn.rhet: The antithesis intensifies the moral reversal.

lit: not honor but dishonor,
sm: not honor but dishonor,

syn.mc: Nominal item in the inheritance list.
syn.svo: Accusative pair after the implied inheritance.
syn.rhet: Honor is inverted into shame.

lit: not freedom but slavery,
sm: not freedom but slavery,

syn.mc: Nominal item in the inheritance list.
syn.svo: Accusative pair after the implied inheritance.
syn.rhet: Political and ethical bondage is named directly.

lit: not kingship but tyranny,
sm: not kingship but tyranny,

syn.mc: Nominal item in the inheritance list.
syn.svo: Accusative pair after the implied inheritance.
syn.rhet: Royal rule is reversed into oppression.

lit: not life but death,
sm: not life but death,

syn.mc: Nominal item in the inheritance list.
syn.svo: Accusative pair after the implied inheritance.
syn.rhet: The climax of the inheritance list turns from civic goods to life itself.

lit: not salvation but destruction.
sm: not salvation but destruction.

syn.mc: Final item in the inheritance list.
syn.svo: Accusative pair after the implied inheritance.
syn.rhet: The list ends with the most comprehensive negation, ruin replacing salvation.

src: καινὴ δὲ καὶ φοβερὰ ἡ τῶν ἀνθρώπων ἐπὶ τῆς γῆς ἐγίνετο ἀπώλεια
lit: And the destruction of men on the earth was becoming new and fearful
sm: And the destruction of humankind on earth was becoming new and dreadful,

syn.mc: Main clause.
syn.svo: Subject: ἡ ... ἀπώλεια. Verb: ἐγίνετο. Predicate adjectives: καινὴ καὶ φοβερὰ. Dependent genitive: τῶν ἀνθρώπων. Locative phrase: ἐπὶ τῆς γῆς.
syn.rhet: The sentence reframes ruin itself as something novel and terrifying.

lit: For these things were happening to them.
sm: For these things were happening to them.
emd: Trailing OCR quotation mark normalized as punctuation.

syn.mc: Explanatory main clause.
syn.svo: Subject: τάδε. Verb: συνέβαινεν. Indirect object: αὐτοῖς.
syn.rhet: A summary sentence links the inherited ruin to the ensuing degradation.

lit: They were being snatched away by tyrannical sin,
sm: They were being snatched away by tyrannical sin,

syn.mc: Main clause in the consequence series begun by line 366.
syn.svo: Subject: they, implied. Verb: ἀνηρπάζοντο. Agent phrase: ὑπὸ τῆς τυραννικῆς ἁμαρτίας.
syn.rhet: Personified sin becomes the tyrant carrying off its victims.

src: καὶ ἤγοντο εἰς τοὺς χώρους τῶν ἐπιθυμιῶν
lit: and they were led into the places of desires
sm: and they were led into the places of desire,

syn.mc: Coordinate clause continuing the consequence series.
syn.svo: Subject: they, implied. Verb: ἤγοντο. Prepositional phrase: εἰς τοὺς χώρους τῶν ἐπιθυμιῶν.
syn.rhet: The victims are depicted as passive captives moved into desire's territory.

src: ἐν οἷς περιηντλοῦντο ὑπὸ τῶν ἀκορέστων ἡδονῶν,
lit: in which they were <UNCERTAIN: harried> by insatiable pleasures,
sm: in which they were driven about by insatiable pleasures,
emd: The verb περιηντλοῦντο is obscure and not securely analyzable; I have left it as transmitted and flagged it explicitly.

syn.mc: Relative clause continuing the chain of captivity and appetite.
syn.sub: ἐν οἷς introduces the location/sphere of the action.
syn.svo: Subject: they, implied. Verb: περιηντλοῦντο (uncertain). Agent phrase: ὑπὸ τῶν ἀκορέστων ἡδονῶν.
syn.rhet: The language moves from capture to immersion in pleasure.

lit: under adultery,
sm: under the sway of adultery,

syn.mc: Fragmentary prepositional phrase continuing the consequence series.
syn.svo: No verb in the line; the phrase depends on the preceding sequence of passive verbs and relative clauses.
syn.rhet: The vice-list culminates in a stark prepositional fragment.
